===''Pokémon the Series''===
[[File:Marlon anime.png|thumb|250px|Marlon in ''[[Pokémon the Series: Black & White]]'']]
====''[[Pokémon the Series: Black & White]]''====
Marlon appeared in ''[[BW094|The Road to Humilau!]]'', where {{un|Cameron}} aimed to challenge his [[Gym]] for his final [[Badge]]. After Cameron and {{Ash}} lost their way to [[Humilau City]], Marlon found the two {{pkmn|Trainer}}s and took them to his Gym. There, he faced Cameron in a [[Gym]] {{pkmn|battle}}. Although Marlon had a powerful [[Party|team]], he was defeated by Cameron's {{p|Samurott}}, giving Cameron his last Gym Badge.

====[[Pokémon movie]]s====
Marlon made a cameo appearance in the ending credits of ''[[M16|Genesect and the Legend Awakened]]''.

====Character====
Marlon is a spirited [[Gym Leader]] with a laidback personality. He has a strong team of {{t|Water}} Pokémon, which perform well in the [[Humilau Gym]]'s open ocean battlefield. Marlon utilizes offense to bring down his challengers and takes advantage of the Gym's aquatic setting.

Afterwards, Mantine faced off against Cameron's female {{p|Samurott}} and was knocked out without landing a single attack on her.
